content: Diagnosis based on serum 25-hydroxyvitamin D [25(OH)D] level - reflects cutaneous synthesis and dietary intake, half-life ~2-3 weeks. 1,25-dihydroxyvitamin D not useful for assessing status (may be normal/elevated in deficiency due to secondary hyperparathyroidism). Classification (controversial, varies by organization): severe deficiency <12 ng/mL (<30 nmol/L); deficiency <20 ng/mL (<50 nmol/L); insufficiency 20-29 ng/mL (50-74 nmol/L); sufficiency ≥30 ng/mL (≥75 nmol/L); potential toxicity >150 ng/mL (>375 nmol/L). IOM considers ≥20 ng/mL adequate for bone health; Endocrine Society recommends ≥30 ng/mL. Screening not recommended for general population; test high-risk groups: osteoporosis, fractures, malabsorption (celiac, IBD, bariatric surgery), obesity, CKD, liver disease, granulomatous diseases, certain medications, limited sun exposure, dark skin. Also measure calcium, phosphorus, alkaline phosphatase, PTH if deficiency suspected.
